revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Ostjapan]][[ja:東日本]][[ko:동일본]][[ru:Восточная Япония]][[uk:Східна Японія]][[zh:東日本]]
{{Otheruses||Asteroid|Eastern Japan (Asteroid)|qq=}} [[File:Japan_Chubu_Region_large.png|thumb|Generally, the eastern side of the light green region is Eastern Japan.]] '''Eastern Japan''' (東日本, Higashi Nihon, Higashi Nippon) refers to regions east of {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|qid=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}}, encompassing the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}}, {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}}, and {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|qid=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}. It is often used to denote the Tōhoku and Kantō regions.<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%9D%B1%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC-608715]</ref> == Definition == In general, Eastern Japan refers to {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|qid=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}, the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}}, and the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}}.{{Refnest|group="note"|It is commonly defined as "the collective term for Kantō, Tōhoku, and Hokkaido regions."<ref>{{ill|Concise Japanese Place Name Dictionary, 3rd Edition|ja|コンサイス日本地名事典 第3版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Concise Japanese Place Name Dictionary, 3rd Edition|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>.|qq=}}{{Refnest|group="note"|Normally, the term encompasses Kantō, Tōhoku, and Hokkaido regions.<ref>{{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Place Names|ja|日本地名大百科 ランドジャポニカ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Encyclopedia of Japanese Place Names|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>.|qq=}} During the mid-Meiji period, with the establishment of the 47 prefectures, the term "Eastern Japan" referred to the administrative divisions of the {{ill|Tōkaidō (region)|en|Tōkaidō (region)|qid=Q1196306|lt=Tōkaidō (region)|1=Tōkaidō (region)}}, {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|qid=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō|1=Tōsandō}}, {{ill|Hokurikudō|en|Hokurikudō|qid=Q3139239|lt=Hokurikudō|1=Hokurikudō}}, and {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|qid=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido (historical)|1=Hokkaido}}. It denoted areas east of Ise Province and Iga Province in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Ōmi Province in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, and Wakasa Province in {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|qid=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture|1=Fukui Prefecture}}. Conversely, regions west of Kyoto and Nara (collectively known as the {{ill|Five provinces of the Kinai|ja|五畿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five provinces of the Kinai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 畿内|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 畿内|1=Kinai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q906708}}) were classified as {{ill|Western Japan|ja|西日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Western Japan|1=Western Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1063946}}. From a geological perspective, the term often refers to areas east of the western boundary of the {{ill|Fossa Magna|en|Fossa Magna|qid=Q5356574|lt=Fossa Magna|1=Fossa Magna}}, which runs through {{ill|Itoigawa|en|Itoigawa|qid=Q857208|lt=Itoigawa|1=Itoigawa}}, the Himekawa River, Aoki Lake, Azumino, Shiojiri Pass, the Kamanashi River, Hayakawa, and the Fuji River.<ref>{{ill|Concise Japanese Place Name Dictionary, 3rd Edition|ja|コンサイス日本地名事典 第3版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Concise Japanese Place Name Dictionary, 3rd Edition|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mext.go.jp/component/a_menu/education/micro_detail/__icsFiles/afieldfile/2011/01/05/1234912_003.pdf|title=Explanation of the Junior High School Curriculum Guidelines: Social Studies|accessdate=2014-04-19|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140712124955/http://www.mext.go.jp/component/a_menu/education/micro_detail/__icsFiles/afieldfile/2011/01/05/1234912_003.pdf|archivedate=2014-07-12|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.u-gakugei.ac.jp/~chiriken/pdf/shakai10.pdf|title=Report on Educational Programs for Elementary School Social Studies at Tokyo Gakugei University, 2010|accessdate=2014-04-19|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140424231208/http://www.u-gakugei.ac.jp/~chiriken/pdf/shakai10.pdf|archivedate=2014-04-24|qq=}}</ref>. Other definitions consider areas east of the {{ill|Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line|en|Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line|qid=Q906591|lt=Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line|1=Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line}}. In linguistics, the {{ill|Eastern Japanese dialects|ja|東日本方言|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Japanese dialects|1=Eastern Japanese|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11527182}} include the dialects of Hokkaido, Tōhoku, Kantō, and parts of the Tōkai-Tōsan dialect spoken in Niigata (excluding Sado Island), Nagano, Gifu, and Aichi.<ref>{{ill|Japanese Dialectology|ja|日本方言学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Dialectology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> Cultural distinctions often define Eastern Japan as areas east of Niigata, Nagano, and Shizuoka Prefectures.<ref>{{ill|Concise Japanese Place Name Dictionary, 3rd Edition|ja|コンサイス日本地名事典 第3版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Concise Japanese Place Name Dictionary, 3rd Edition|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref><ref>{{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Place Names|ja|日本地名大百科 ランドジャポニカ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Encyclopedia of Japanese Place Names|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> In weather forecasting, Eastern Japan is categorized alongside Northern Japan, Western Japan, and Okinawa/Amami. It typically includes Kantō, Kōshin, Hokuriku, and Tōkai (including Mie Prefecture), while Hokkaido and Tōhoku are classified as Northern Japan.<ref>[https://www.jma.go.jp/jma/kishou/know/yougo_hp/tiikimei.html Japan Meteorological Agency Regional Names]</ref>. == Population == As of the 2020 census, Eastern Japan (comprising Hokkaido, Tōhoku, and Kantō) has a population of 57.49 million, accounting for 45.6% of Japan's total population.<ref>[https://www.stat.go.jp/data/kokusei/2020/kekka/pdf/outline_01.pdf]</ref> === Total Fertility Rate === The total fertility rate in Eastern Japan is lower than the national average. In 2021, even Fukushima Prefecture—the highest in the region—Ranked only 28th nationwide, with all prefectures falling within the bottom 20.<ref>[https://www.mhlw.go.jp/toukei/saikin/hw/jinkou/geppo/nengai21/dl/h9.pdf]</ref> Historically, until the early 2000s, Eastern Japan (excluding Tokyo and Hokkaido) did not significantly differ from the national average, with Yamagata and Fukushima occasionally Ranking second after Okinawa. However, since 2006, as the national average recovered, Eastern Japan—especially Kantō and Tōhoku—has lagged, resulting in a pronounced East-West disparity. == Notable Organizations with "Eastern Japan" in Their Names == === Reflecting Regional Operations === * {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} (JR East) - Operating primarily in Niigata, Nagano, Shizuoka (parts of Izu), and Yamanashi, as well as throughout Kantō and Tōhoku. Hokkaido operations are managed separately by {{ill|Hokkaido Railway Company|en|Hokkaido Railway Company|qid=Q498930|lt=Hokkaido Railway Company|1=Hokkaido Railway Company}}. * {{ill|NTT East|ja|東日本電信電話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NTT East|1=Q11527212|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11527212}} - Covers Niigata, Nagano, and Yamanashi, as well as areas north and east of Kanagawa and parts of Shizuoka (Atami and Susono). * {{ill|East Nippon Expressway Company|en|East Nippon Expressway Company|qid=Q1068351|lt=East Nippon Expressway Company|1=East Nippon Expressway Company}} (NEXCO East) - Manages areas east of Gunma, Saitama, and Chiba, excluding the Tōmei and Chūō Expressway corridors.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kantō|ja|関東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō|1=Kantō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11655841}} * {{ill|Tōhoku|ja|東北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東北地方|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東北地方|1=Tōhoku region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q129465}} * {{ill|Fossa Magna|en|Fossa Magna|qid=Q5356574|lt=Fossa Magna|1=Fossa Magna}} * {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|qid=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}} * {{ill|East-West demographic divide in Japan|ja|東西の人口格差|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East-West demographic divide in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|qid=Q1136168|lt=Eastern Japan Great Earthquake|1=Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}} {{Japan Regions|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|東日本|version=104259174|comment=Imported full ja history}}
